Winner.Name: Jacobus H. van 't Hoff
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by the discovery of the laws of chemical dynamics and osmotic pressure in solutions

Winner.Name: Sully Prudhomme
Winner.Motivation: in special recognition of his poetic composition, which gives evidence of lofty idealism, artistic perfection and a rare combination of the qualities of both heart and intellect

Winner.Name: Henry Dunant
Winner.Motivation: for his humanitarian efforts to help wounded soldiers and create international understanding

Winner.Name: Frédéric Passy
Winner.Motivation: for his lifelong work for international peace conferences, diplomacy and arbitration

Winner.Name: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by the discovery of the remarkable rays subsequently named after him

Winner.Name: Emil von Behring
Winner.Motivation: for his work on serum therapy, especially its application against diphtheria, by which he has opened a new road in the domain of medical science and thereby placed in the hands of the physician a victorious weapon against illness and deaths

Winner.Name: Emil Fischer
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by his work on sugar and purine syntheses

Winner.Name: Theodor Mommsen
Winner.Motivation: the greatest living master of the art of historical writing, with special reference to his monumental work, A history of Rome

Winner.Name: Élie Ducommun
Winner.Motivation: for his untiring and skilful directorship of the Bern Peace Bureau

Winner.Name: Albert Gobat
Winner.Motivation: for his eminently practical administration of the Inter-Parliamentary Union

Winner.Name: Hendrik A. Lorentz
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary service they rendered by their researches into the influence of magnetism upon radiation phenomena

Winner.Name: Pieter Zeeman
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary service they rendered by their researches into the influence of magnetism upon radiation phenomena

Winner.Name: Ronald Ross
Winner.Motivation: for his work on malaria, by which he has shown how it enters the organism and thereby has laid the foundation for successful research on this disease and methods of combating it

Winner.Name: Svante Arrhenius
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered to the advancement of chemistry by his electrolytic theory of dissociation

Winner.Name: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson
Winner.Motivation: as a tribute to his noble, magnificent and versatile poetry, which has always been distinguished by both the freshness of its inspiration and the rare purity of its spirit

Winner.Name: Randal Cremer
Winner.Motivation: for his longstanding and devoted effort in favour of the ideas of peace and arbitration

Winner.Name: Henri Becquerel
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by his discovery of spontaneous radioactivity

Winner.Name: Pierre Curie
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services they have rendered by their joint researches on the radiation phenomena discovered by Professor Henri Becquerel

Winner.Name: Marie Curie
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the extraordinary services they have rendered by their joint researches on the radiation phenomena discovered by Professor Henri Becquerel

Winner.Name: Niels Ryberg Finsen
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his contribution to the treatment of diseases, especially lupus vulgaris, with concentrated light radiation, whereby he has opened a new avenue for medical science

Winner.Name: Sir William Ramsay
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his services in the discovery of the inert gaseous elements in air, and his determination of their place in the periodic system

Winner.Name: Frédéric Mistral
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the fresh originality and true inspiration of his poetic production, which faithfully reflects the natural scenery and native spirit of his people, and, in addition, his significant work as a Provençal philologist

Winner.Name: José Echegaray
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the numerous and brilliant compositions which, in an individual and original manner, have revived the great traditions of the Spanish drama
for its striving in public law to develop peaceful ties between nations and to make the laws of war more humane

Winner.Name: Lord Rayleigh
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations of the densities of the most important gases and for his discovery of argon in connection with these studies

Winner.Name: Ivan Pavlov
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work on the physiology of digestion, through which knowledge on vital aspects of the subject has been transformed and enlarged

Winner.Name: Adolf von Baeyer
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his services in the advancement of organic chemistry and the chemical industry, through his work on organic dyes and hydroaromatic compounds

Winner.Name: Henryk Sienkiewicz
Winner.Motivation: because of his outstanding merits as an epic writer

Winner.Name: Bertha von Suttner
Winner.Motivation: for her audacity to oppose the horrors of war

Winner.Name: Philipp Lenard
Winner.Motivation: for his work on cathode rays

Winner.Name: Robert Koch
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations and discoveries in relation to tuberculosis

Winner.Name: Henri Moissan
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the great services rendered by him in his investigation and isolation of the element fluorine, and for the adoption in the service of science of the electric furnace called after him

Winner.Name: Giosuè Carducci
Winner.Motivation: not only in consideration of his deep learning and critical research, but above all as a tribute to the creative energy, freshness of style, and lyrical force which characterize his poetic masterpieces

Winner.Name: Theodore Roosevelt
Winner.Motivation: for his role in bringing to an end the bloody war recently waged between two of the world's great powers, Japan and Russia

Winner.Name: J.J. Thomson
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the great merits of his theoretical and experimental investigations on the conduction of electricity by gases

Winner.Name: Camillo Golgi
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their work on the structure of the nervous system

Winner.Name: Santiago Ramón y Cajal
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their work on the structure of the nervous system

Winner.Name: Eduard Buchner
Winner.Motivation: for his biochemical researches and his discovery of cell-free fermentation

Winner.Name: Rudyard Kipling
Winner.Motivation: in consideration of the power of observation, originality of imagination, virility of ideas and remarkable talent for narration which characterize the creations of this world-famous author

Winner.Name: Ernesto Teodoro Moneta
Winner.Motivation: for his work in the press and in peace meetings, both public and private, for an understanding between France and Italy

Winner.Name: Louis Renault
Winner.Motivation: for his decisive influence upon the conduct and outcome of the Hague and Geneva Conferences

Winner.Name: Albert A. Michelson
Winner.Motivation: for his optical precision instruments and the spectroscopic and metrological investigations carried out with their aid

Winner.Name: Alphonse Laveran
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work on the role played by protozoa in causing diseases

Winner.Name: Ernest Rutherford
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations into the disintegration of the elements, and the chemistry of radioactive substances

Winner.Name: Rudolf Eucken
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his earnest search for truth, his penetrating power of thought, his wide range of vision, and the warmth and strength in presentation with which in his numerous works he has vindicated and developed an idealistic philosophy of life

Winner.Name: Klas Pontus Arnoldson
Winner.Motivation: for their long time work for the cause of peace as politicians, peace society leaders, orators and authors

Winner.Name: Fredrik Bajer
Winner.Motivation: for their long time work for the cause of peace as politicians, peace society leaders, orators and authors

Winner.Name: Gabriel Lippmann
Winner.Motivation: for his method of reproducing colours photographically based on the phenomenon of interference

Winner.Name: Ilya Mechnikov
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their work on immunity

Winner.Name: Paul Ehrlich
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their work on immunity

Winner.Name: Wilhelm Ostwald
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work on catalysis and for his investigations into the fundamental principles governing chemical equilibria and rates of reaction

Winner.Name: Selma Lagerlöf
Winner.Motivation: in appreciation of the lofty idealism, vivid imagination and spiritual perception that characterize her writings

Winner.Name: Auguste Beernaert
Winner.Motivation: for their prominent position in the international movement for peace and arbitration

Winner.Name: Paul Henri d'Estournelles de Constant
Winner.Motivation: for their prominent position in the international movement for peace and arbitration

Winner.Name: Guglielmo Marconi
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their contributions to the development of wireless telegraphy

Winner.Name: Ferdinand Braun
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their contributions to the development of wireless telegraphy

Winner.Name: Theodor Kocher
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the physiology, pathology and surgery of the thyroid gland

Winner.Name: Otto Wallach
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his services to organic chemistry and the chemical industry by his pioneer work in the field of alicyclic compounds

Winner.Name: Paul Heyse
Winner.Motivation: as a tribute to the consummate artistry, permeated with idealism, which he has demonstrated during his long productive career as a lyric poet, dramatist, novelist and writer of world-renowned short stories
for acting as a link between the peace societies of the various countries, and helping them to organize the world rallies of the international peace movement

Winner.Name: Johannes Diderik van der Waals
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the equation of state for gases and liquids

Winner.Name: Albrecht Kossel
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the contributions to our knowledge of cell chemistry made through his work on proteins, including the nucleic substances

Winner.Name: Marie Curie
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of her services to the advancement of chemistry by the discovery of the elements radium and polonium, by the isolation of radium and the study of the nature and compounds of this remarkable element

Winner.Name: Maurice Maeterlinck
Winner.Motivation: in appreciation of his many-sided literary activities, and especially of his dramatic works, which are distinguished by a wealth of imagination and by a poetic fancy, which reveals, sometimes in the guise of a fairy tale, a deep inspiration, while in a mysterious way they appeal to the readers' own feelings and stimulate their imaginations

Winner.Name: Tobias Asser
Winner.Motivation: for his role as co-founder of the Institut de droit international, initiator of the Conferences on International Private Law (Conférences de Droit international privé) at the Hague, and pioneer in the field of international legal relations

Winner.Name: Alfred Fried
Winner.Motivation: for his effort to expose and fight what he considers to be the main cause of war, namely, the anarchy in international relations

Winner.Name: Wilhelm Wien
Winner.Motivation: for his discoveries regarding the laws governing the radiation of heat

Winner.Name: Allvar Gullstrand
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the dioptrics of the eye

Winner.Name: Victor Grignard
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of the so-called Grignard reagent, which in recent years has greatly advanced the progress of organic chemistry

Winner.Name: Paul Sabatier
Winner.Motivation: for his method of hydrogenating organic compounds in the presence of finely disintegrated metals whereby the progress of organic chemistry has been greatly advanced in recent years

Winner.Name: Gerhart Hauptmann
Winner.Motivation: primarily in recognition of his fruitful, varied and outstanding production in the realm of dramatic art

Winner.Name: Elihu Root
Winner.Motivation: for bringing about better understanding between the countries of North and South America and initiating important arbitration agreements between the United States and other countries

Winner.Name: Gustaf Dalén
Winner.Motivation: for his invention of automatic regulators for use in conjunction with gas accumulators for illuminating lighthouses and buoys

Winner.Name: Alexis Carrel
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work on vascular suture and the transplantation of blood vessels and organs

Winner.Name: Alfred Werner
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work on the linkage of atoms in molecules by which he has thrown new light on earlier investigations and opened up new fields of research especially in inorganic chemistry

Winner.Name: Rabindranath Tagore
Winner.Motivation: because of his profoundly sensitive, fresh and beautiful verse, by which, with consummate skill, he has made his poetic thought, expressed in his own English words, a part of the literature of the West

Winner.Name: Henri La Fontaine
Winner.Motivation: for his unparalleled contribution to the organization of peaceful internationalism

Winner.Name: Heike Kamerlingh Onnes
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations on the properties of matter at low temperatures which led, inter alia, to the production of liquid helium

Winner.Name: Charles Richet
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work on anaphylaxis

Winner.Name: Theodore W. Richards
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his accurate determinations of the atomic weight of a large number of chemical elements

Winner.Name: Max von Laue
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the diffraction of X-rays by crystals

Winner.Name: Robert Bárány
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the physiology and pathology of the vestibular apparatus

Winner.Name: Richard Willstätter
Winner.Motivation: for his researches on plant pigments, especially chlorophyll

Winner.Name: Romain Rolland
Winner.Motivation: as a tribute to the lofty idealism of his literary production and to the sympathy and love of truth with which he has described different types of human beings

Winner.Name: William Bragg
Winner.Motivation: for their services in the analysis of crystal structure by means of X-rays

Winner.Name: Lawrence Bragg
Winner.Motivation: for their services in the analysis of crystal structure by means of X-rays

Winner.Name: Verner von Heidenstam
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his significance as the leading representative of a new era in our literature

Winner.Name: Karl Gjellerup
Winner.Motivation: for his varied and rich poetry, which is inspired by lofty ideals

Winner.Name: Henrik Pontoppidan
Winner.Motivation: for his authentic descriptions of present-day life in Denmark
for the efforts to take care of wounded soldiers and prisoners of war and their families

Winner.Name: Charles Glover Barkla
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the characteristic Röntgen radiation of the elements

Winner.Name: Fritz Haber
Winner.Motivation: for the synthesis of ammonia from its elements

Winner.Name: Max Planck
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the services he rendered to the advancement of Physics by his discovery of energy quanta

Winner.Name: Carl Spitteler
Winner.Motivation: in special appreciation of his epic, Olympian Spring

Winner.Name: Woodrow Wilson
Winner.Motivation: for his role as founder of the League of Nations

Winner.Name: Johannes Stark
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the Doppler effect in canal rays and the splitting of spectral lines in electric fields

Winner.Name: Jules Bordet
Winner.Motivation: for his discoveries relating to immunity

Winner.Name: Walther Nernst
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his work in thermochemistry

Winner.Name: Knut Hamsun
Winner.Motivation: for his monumental work, Growth of the Soil

Winner.Name: Léon Bourgeois
Winner.Motivation: for his longstanding contribution to the cause of peace and justice and his prominent role in the establishment of the League of Nations

Winner.Name: Charles Edouard Guillaume
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of the service he has rendered to precision measurements in Physics by his discovery of anomalies in nickel steel alloys

Winner.Name: August Krogh
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the capillary motor regulating mechanism

Winner.Name: Frederick Soddy
Winner.Motivation: for his contributions to our knowledge of the chemistry of radioactive substances, and his investigations into the origin and nature of isotopes

Winner.Name: Anatole France
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his brilliant literary achievements, characterized as they are by a nobility of style, a profound human sympathy, grace, and a true Gallic temperament

Winner.Name: Hjalmar Branting
Winner.Motivation: for their lifelong contributions to the cause of peace and organized internationalism

Winner.Name: Christian Lange
Winner.Motivation: for their lifelong contributions to the cause of peace and organized internationalism

Winner.Name: Albert Einstein
Winner.Motivation: for his services to Theoretical Physics, and especially for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect

Winner.Name: Francis W. Aston
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery, by means of his mass spectrograph, of isotopes, in a large number of non-radioactive elements, and for his enunciation of the whole-number rule

Winner.Name: Jacinto Benavente
Winner.Motivation: for the happy manner in which he has continued the illustrious traditions of the Spanish drama

Winner.Name: Fridtjof Nansen
Winner.Motivation: for his leading role in the repatriation of prisoners of war, in international relief work and as the League of Nations' High Commissioner for refugees

Winner.Name: Niels Bohr
Winner.Motivation: for his services in the investigation of the structure of atoms and of the radiation emanating from them

Winner.Name: Archibald V. Hill
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery relating to the production of heat in the muscle

Winner.Name: Otto Meyerhof
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the fixed relationship between the consumption of oxygen and the metabolism of lactic acid in the muscle

Winner.Name: Fritz Pregl
Winner.Motivation: for his invention of the method of micro-analysis of organic substances

Winner.Name: William Butler Yeats
Winner.Motivation: for his always inspired poetry, which in a highly artistic form gives expression to the spirit of a whole nation

Winner.Name: Robert A. Millikan
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the elementary charge of electricity and on the photoelectric effect

Winner.Name: Frederick G. Banting
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of insulin

Winner.Name: John Macleod
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of insulin

Winner.Name: Wladyslaw Reymont
Winner.Motivation: for his great national epic, The Peasants

Winner.Name: Manne Siegbahn
Winner.Motivation: for his discoveries and research in the field of X-ray spectroscopy

Winner.Name: Willem Einthoven
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the mechanism of the electrocardiogram

Winner.Name: Richard Zsigmondy
Winner.Motivation: for his demonstration of the heterogenous nature of colloid solutions and for the methods he used, which have since become fundamental in modern colloid chemistry

Winner.Name: George Bernard Shaw
Winner.Motivation: for his work which is marked by both idealism and humanity, its stimulating satire often being infused with a singular poetic beauty

Winner.Name: Sir Austen Chamberlain
Winner.Motivation: for his crucial role in bringing about the Locarno Treaty

Winner.Name: Charles G. Dawes
Winner.Motivation: for his crucial role in bringing about the Dawes Plan

Winner.Name: James Franck
Winner.Motivation: for their discovery of the laws governing the impact of an electron upon an atom

Winner.Name: Gustav Hertz
Winner.Motivation: for their discovery of the laws governing the impact of an electron upon an atom

Winner.Name: The Svedberg
Winner.Motivation: for his work on disperse systems

Winner.Name: Grazia Deledda
Winner.Motivation: for her idealistically inspired writings which with plastic clarity picture the life on her native island and with depth and sympathy deal with human problems in general

Winner.Name: Aristide Briand
Winner.Motivation: for their crucial role in bringing about the Locarno Treaty

Winner.Name: Gustav Stresemann
Winner.Motivation: for their crucial role in bringing about the Locarno Treaty

Winner.Name: Jean Baptiste Perrin
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the discontinuous structure of matter, and especially for his discovery of sedimentation equilibrium

Winner.Name: Johannes Fibiger
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the Spiroptera carcinoma

Winner.Name: Heinrich Wieland
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations of the constitution of the bile acids and related substances

Winner.Name: Henri Bergson
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of his rich and vitalizing ideas and the brilliant skill with which they have been presented

Winner.Name: Ferdinand Buisson
Winner.Motivation: for their contribution to the emergence in France and Germany of a public opinion which favours peaceful international cooperation

Winner.Name: Ludwig Quidde
Winner.Motivation: for their contribution to the emergence in France and Germany of a public opinion which favours peaceful international cooperation

Winner.Name: Arthur H. Compton
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the effect named after him

Winner.Name: C.T.R. Wilson
Winner.Motivation: for his method of making the paths of electrically charged particles visible by condensation of vapour

Winner.Name: Julius Wagner-Jauregg
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the therapeutic value of malaria inoculation in the treatment of dementia paralytica

Winner.Name: Adolf Windaus
Winner.Motivation: for the services rendered through his research into the constitution of the sterols and their connection with the vitamins

Winner.Name: Sigrid Undset
Winner.Motivation: principally for her powerful descriptions of Northern life during the Middle Ages

Winner.Name: Owen Willans Richardson
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the thermionic phenomenon and especially for the discovery of the law named after him

Winner.Name: Charles Nicolle
Winner.Motivation: for his work on typhus

Winner.Name: Arthur Harden
Winner.Motivation: for their investigations on the fermentation of sugar and fermentative enzymes

Winner.Name: Hans von Euler-Chelpin
Winner.Motivation: for their investigations on the fermentation of sugar and fermentative enzymes

Winner.Name: Thomas Mann
Winner.Motivation: principally for his great novel, Buddenbrooks, which has won steadily increased recognition as one of the classic works of contemporary literature

Winner.Name: Frank B. Kellogg
Winner.Motivation: for his crucial role in bringing about the Briand-Kellogg Pact

Winner.Name: Louis de Broglie
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the wave nature of electrons

Winner.Name: Christiaan Eijkman
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the antineuritic vitamin

Winner.Name: Sir Frederick Hopkins
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the growth-stimulating vitamins

Winner.Name: Hans Fischer
Winner.Motivation: for his researches into the constitution of haemin and chlorophyll and especially for his synthesis of haemin

Winner.Name: Sinclair Lewis
Winner.Motivation: for his vigorous and graphic art of description and his ability to create, with wit and humour, new types of characters

Winner.Name: Nathan Söderblom
Winner.Motivation: for promoting Christian unity and helping create 'that new attitude of mind which is necessary if peace between nations is to become reality'

Winner.Name: Sir Chandrasekhara Venkata Raman
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the scattering of light and for the discovery of the effect named after him

Winner.Name: Karl Landsteiner
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of human blood groups

Winner.Name: Carl Bosch
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their contributions to the invention and development of chemical high pressure methods

Winner.Name: Friedrich Bergius
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their contributions to the invention and development of chemical high pressure methods

Winner.Name: Erik Axel Karlfeldt
Winner.Motivation: The poetry of Erik Axel Karlfeldt

Winner.Name: Jane Addams
Winner.Motivation: for their assiduous effort to revive the ideal of peace and to rekindle the spirit of peace in their own nation and in the whole of mankind

Winner.Name: Nicholas Murray Butler
Winner.Motivation: for their assiduous effort to revive the ideal of peace and to rekindle the spirit of peace in their own nation and in the whole of mankind

Winner.Name: Otto Warburg
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the nature and mode of action of the respiratory enzyme

Winner.Name: Irving Langmuir
Winner.Motivation: for his discoveries and investigations in surface chemistry

Winner.Name: John Galsworthy
Winner.Motivation: for his distinguished art of narration which takes its highest form in The Forsyte Saga

Winner.Name: Werner Heisenberg
Winner.Motivation: for the creation of quantum mechanics, the application of which has, inter alia, led to the discovery of the allotropic forms of hydrogen

Winner.Name: Sir Charles Sherrington
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries regarding the functions of neurons

Winner.Name: Edgar Adrian
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries regarding the functions of neurons

Winner.Name: Ivan Bunin
Winner.Motivation: for the strict artistry with which he has carried on the classical Russian traditions in prose writing

Winner.Name: Sir Norman Angell
Winner.Motivation: for having exposed by his pen the illusion of war and presented a convincing plea for international cooperation and peace

Winner.Name: Erwin Schrödinger
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of new productive forms of atomic theory

Winner.Name: Paul A.M. Dirac
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of new productive forms of atomic theory

Winner.Name: Thomas H. Morgan
Winner.Motivation: for his discoveries concerning the role played by the chromosome in heredity

Winner.Name: Harold C. Urey
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of heavy hydrogen

Winner.Name: Luigi Pirandello
Winner.Motivation: for his bold and ingenious revival of dramatic and scenic art

Winner.Name: Arthur Henderson
Winner.Motivation: for his untiring struggle and his courageous efforts as Chairman of the League of Nations Disarmament Conference 1931-34

Winner.Name: George H. Whipple
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries concerning liver therapy in cases of anaemia

Winner.Name: George R. Minot
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries concerning liver therapy in cases of anaemia

Winner.Name: William P. Murphy
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries concerning liver therapy in cases of anaemia

Winner.Name: Frédéric Joliot
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their synthesis of new radioactive elements

Winner.Name: Irène Joliot-Curie
Winner.Motivation: in recognition of their synthesis of new radioactive elements

Winner.Name: Carl von Ossietzky
Winner.Motivation: for his burning love for freedom of thought and expression and his valuable contribution to the cause of peace

Winner.Name: James Chadwick
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of the neutron

Winner.Name: Hans Spemann
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the organizer effect in embryonic development

Winner.Name: Peter Debye
Winner.Motivation: for his contributions to our knowledge of molecular structure through his investigations on dipole moments and on the diffraction of X-rays and electrons in gases

Winner.Name: Eugene O'Neill
Winner.Motivation: for the power, honesty and deep-felt emotions of his dramatic works, which embody an original concept of tragedy

Winner.Name: Carlos Saavedra Lamas
Winner.Motivation: for his role as father of the Argentine Antiwar Pact of 1933, which he also used as a means to mediate peace between Paraguay and Bolivia in 1935

Winner.Name: Victor F. Hess
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of cosmic radiation

Winner.Name: Carl D. Anderson
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the positron

Winner.Name: Sir Henry Dale
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries relating to chemical transmission of nerve impulses

Winner.Name: Otto Loewi
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries relating to chemical transmission of nerve impulses

Winner.Name: Norman Haworth
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations on carbohydrates and vitamin C

Winner.Name: Paul Karrer
Winner.Motivation: for his investigations on carotenoids, flavins and vitamins A and B2

Winner.Name: Roger Martin du Gard
Winner.Motivation: for the artistic power and truth with which he has depicted human conflict as well as some fundamental aspects of contemporary life in his novel-cycle Les Thibault

Winner.Name: Robert Cecil, Viscount Cecil of Chelwood
Winner.Motivation: for his tireless effort in support of the League of Nations, disarmament and peace

Winner.Name: Clinton Davisson
Winner.Motivation: for their experimental discovery of the diffraction of electrons by crystals

Winner.Name: George Paget Thomson
Winner.Motivation: for their experimental discovery of the diffraction of electrons by crystals

Winner.Name: Albert Szent-Györgyi
Winner.Motivation: for his discoveries in connection with the biological combustion processes, with special reference to vitamin C and the catalysis of fumaric acid

Winner.Name: Richard Kuhn
Winner.Motivation: for his work on carotenoids and vitamins

Winner.Name: Pearl Buck
Winner.Motivation: for her rich and truly epic descriptions of peasant life in China and for her biographical masterpieces
for having carried on the work of Fridtjof Nansen to the benefit of refugees across Europe

Winner.Name: Enrico Fermi
Winner.Motivation: for his demonstrations of the existence of new radioactive elements produced by neutron irradiation, and for his related discovery of nuclear reactions brought about by slow neutrons

Winner.Name: Corneille Heymans
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of the role played by the sinus and aortic mechanisms in the regulation of respiration

Winner.Name: Adolf Butenandt
Winner.Motivation: for his work on sex hormones

Winner.Name: Leopold Ruzicka
Winner.Motivation: for his work on polymethylenes and higher terpenes

Winner.Name: Frans Eemil Sillanpää
Winner.Motivation: for his deep understanding of his country's peasantry and the exquisite art with which he has portrayed their way of life and their relationship with Nature

Winner.Name: Ernest Lawrence
Winner.Motivation: for the invention and development of the cyclotron and for results obtained with it, especially with regard to artificial radioactive elements

Winner.Name: Gerhard Domagk
Winner.Motivation: for the discovery of the antibacterial effects of prontosil

Winner.Name: George de Hevesy
Winner.Motivation: for his work on the use of isotopes as tracers in the study of chemical processes

Winner.Name: Otto Stern
Winner.Motivation: for his contribution to the development of the molecular ray method and his discovery of the magnetic moment of the proton

Winner.Name: Henrik Dam
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of vitamin K

Winner.Name: Edward A. Doisy
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the chemical nature of vitamin K

Winner.Name: Otto Hahn
Winner.Motivation: for his discovery of the fission of heavy nuclei

Winner.Name: Johannes V. Jensen
Winner.Motivation: for the rare strength and fertility of his poetic imagination with which is combined an intellectual curiosity of wide scope and a bold, freshly creative style
for the great work it has performed during the war on behalf of humanity

Winner.Name: Isidor Isaac Rabi
Winner.Motivation: for his resonance method for recording the magnetic properties of atomic nuclei

Winner.Name: Joseph Erlanger
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries relating to the highly differentiated functions of single nerve fibres

Winner.Name: Herbert S. Gasser
Winner.Motivation: for their discoveries relating to the highly differentiated functions of single nerve fibres

Winner.Name: Artturi Virtanen
Winner.Motivation: for his research and inventions in agricultural and nutrition chemistry, especially for his fodder preservation method

Winner.Name: Gabriela Mistral
Winner.Motivation: for her lyric poetry which, inspired by powerful emotions, has made her name a symbol of the idealistic aspirations of the entire Latin American world

Winner.Name: Cordell Hull

Winner.Motivation: The Peace Prize laureates represent civil society in their home countries. They have for many years promoted the right to criticise power and protect the fundamental rights of citizens. They have made an outstanding effort to document war crimes, human right abuses and the abuse of power. Together they demonstrate the significance of civil society for peace and democracy.
